title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
problem_statement: Merge two sorted linked lists and return the result as a sorted list.

examples.explanation: The merged list contains all nodes in sorted order.

constraints: The number of nodes in both lists is in the range [0, 50]
intuition: Walk both lists in parallel and append the smaller node to the merged result.

approaches.name: Iterative merge
approaches.description: Use a dummy head and compare nodes from both lists, attaching the smaller one each step.
approaches.why_not: Concatenating and sorting values is slower and loses the linked structure.

interview_qa.a: It simplifies edge cases by providing a stable start for the merged list.

interview_qa.q: What if one list is empty?
interview_qa.a: The function simply returns the other list unchanged.
